decision_rule_text: n_pass <= 3 -> FAIL -> DIAGNOSTIC ONLY view, no actionable conviction/probability

criterion_4_t_positive_lc_full_any_horiz.reason: no horizon meets positive-sign AND t>1.65 jointly; LC_FULL 10Y excluded as insufficient sample (n=42 << 5*HAC_lag=595)

criterion_5_adf_all_components.threshold: ADF rejects null (p<0.05) for all 5 components
criterion_5_adf_all_components.reason: z1, z4, z5 do not reject the unit-root null at 5%

criterion_6_max_vif_lt_5.reason: z5 VIF = 7.58 and z1 VIF = 6.02 exceed the 5.0 multicollinearity threshold

criterion_7_bonferroni_sig_any_cell.reason: the only Bonferroni-significant cell (z1 NetFed @ 10Y) has n=42 << 5*HAC_lag=595 and is flagged insufficient sample per DECISIONS.md 2026-05-25 Q4; no adequately-sampled cell meets the Bonferroni threshold

calibration_evidence: PIT KS p < 0.0001 across all 12 cells; Gaussian forecast distribution universally rejected; CRPS skill mostly negative (prevailing-mean benchmark beats model)

research_findings_publishable.summary: LC_DEEP beta negative robust at 3Y horizon on 45-year sample; LC_FULL @ 3Y also negative (post-zero-fill)
research_findings_publishable.interpretation: Consistent with credit-cycle / dollar-cycle reversal literature (Fama-French 1988; Schularick-Taylor 2012; Bruno-Shin 2015; Adrian-Boyarchenko 2012). Modern macro-liquidity proxies historically PRECEDE lower forward equity returns at the end-of-cycle, opposite to pre-reg §4.1 priors based on simple loose-money/returns intuition.

research_findings_publishable.summary: All 5 components show NEGATIVE beta at all 4 horizons (z4 at 10Y near zero); sign anomaly is component-level not composite-construction
research_findings_publishable.interpretation: Same mean-reversion / over-extension literature as Finding 1. Methodological lesson: pre-reg priors based on 1970s-90s monetarist intuition fail at multi-year horizons in the modern (post-2008) regime.

research_findings_publishable.summary: Gaussian conditional forecast distribution rejected universally by PIT K-S test (p<0.0001 in all 12 cells) and outperformed by prevailing-mean benchmark (CRPS skill mostly negative)
research_findings_publishable.interpretation: Well-documented limitation of conditional-Gaussian forecast distributions for equity returns (Mandelbrot 1963; Fama 1965; Cont 2001). Methodological lesson: even methodologically-clean predictive composites built on multiple-decade samples fail distributional calibration when the assumed conditional distribution is misspecified. v11.4+ should default to skewed-t (Hansen 1994) or empirical-kernel distributions.

bai_perron_structural_breaks.note: 2020-03 (COVID) and 2023-07 break dates appear in all 3 composites; 2009-09 (post-GFC) appears in LC_TIER2 and LC_DEEP. Economically interpretable break dates.

v11_4_amendment_candidates: Pre-reg §4.1 priors to incorporate both monetarist and mean-reversion literature streams with separate prior probabilities for each component sign
Criterion 4 wording to disambiguate sign vs magnitude requirement explicitly
Conditional probability framework to default to skewed-t (Hansen 1994) or empirical kernel distributions (non-Gaussian) given universal calibration failure documented here
Insufficient-sample gate (n_obs_insample < 5 * HAC_lag) to be an explicit pre-reg condition rather than a post-hoc filter

sessions.scope: Stage 0 deploy + Stage 1 SVG NaN diagnosis
Stage 3 LC v1.0 data layer (A1 + A2)
Stage 3 LC v1.0 modeling layer (A1-ICEDXY + B + C + D + E)
Bootstrap ICE DXY + build artifacts + 12-cell regression
DECISIONS + investigation + F (50K bootstrap + CY) + G (calibration)
